infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE: build.js. Workspace md5 c3ad8c95 vs main md5 12d95af0 (NEW hash — yesterday it was dbd0e96c). Latest GitHub commit 2026-04-15T16:37:03Z added git gc --auto to publish loop for disk-bloat prevention. Workspace has never received ANY of the 3+ commits to build.js since 2026-04-10. This is the 3rd consecutive report flagging build.js staleness.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum build.js (workspace) c3ad8c95 vs curl raw main 12d95af0. GitHub commits API: latest commit 2026-04-15T16:37:03Z 'Disk-bloat prevention: git gc in publish + archive-old-reports utility'.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: build.js is not in its own OWNERSHIP table. The publish loop iterates OWNERSHIP keys and copies git-owned files to workspace; build.js is not a key, so it never copies itself.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: PROP-007 (unchanged from yesterday). The decider needs to implement this proposal.

infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE: build-scripts/generate-html.js. Workspace md5 f8654941 vs main md5 88402006. NEW staleness — first time this file is flagged. Latest commit 2026-04-15T17:00:44Z 'ISS-1035: Add WIN-068 to eclipse magnetometer dedup cluster'. This file generates the HTML site; any agent or manual run using the workspace copy will produce stale HTML.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum build-scripts/generate-html.js (workspace) f8654941 vs curl raw main 88402006.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: generate-html.js IS in OWNERSHIP as git-owned, so this should be copied on publish. Either (a) no publish has run since the commit, or (b) the publish ran but the sync step failed/skipped. Since build.js itself is stale, if a publish ran using the stale workspace build.js (unlikely — decider clones fresh), it would use an old OWNERSHIP that might not have this file. More likely: the publish ran from a clone that has the OWNERSHIP entry, but build.js's copyFileSync for generate-html.js encountered a FUSE glitch.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: Check whether the next publish properly syncs this file. If it persists another day, the sync step may need a retry/verify loop.

infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE CARRYOVER: build-scripts/digest-reviews.js. Workspace md5 0a11b762 vs main md5 6a742c5e. 3rd consecutive report. The exit-code-2 fix from 2026-04-15T05:23Z is still not in the workspace. Not classified in OWNERSHIP, so publish never copies it.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum build-scripts/digest-reviews.js (workspace) 0a11b762 vs curl raw main 6a742c5e.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: Not in OWNERSHIP. PROP-007 covers this.

infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE: docs/index.html. Workspace md5 cf859205 vs main md5 db5d9288. Latest commit 2026-04-16T03:42:14Z 'Decider self-apply: 5 patches'. This file IS in OWNERSHIP as git-owned. Same mystery as generate-html.js: either no publish since the commit, or the sync step encountered an issue.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum docs/index.html (workspace) cf859205 vs curl raw main db5d9288.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: Likely: the decider committed at 03:42Z but the next publish hasn't run yet, or publish ran but the sync step partially failed. generate-html.js and docs/index.html are both OWNERSHIP-classified, so their staleness is NOT caused by the PROP-007 gap — it's a different mechanism.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: Monitor next publish run. If both classified files sync correctly, this is a timing issue. If they remain stale after the next publish, investigate the sync step's error handling.

infrastructure_findings.description: DISK AT 83% USED, 1715MB free. Trajectory: 79% (04-13) → 81% (04-14) → 82% (04-15) → 83% (04-16). At ~1pp/day, 90% critical in ~7 days. Good news: new build.js commit added git gc --auto to publish, which should slow pack-file growth. Disk breakdown: /usr 6.1G (system, non-reducible), /var 1.4G, /sessions 62M, /tmp 98M. The growth is in /var, not monitor/ (which is only 16MB total).
infrastructure_findings.evidence: df / → 83% used, 1715MB free. du -sh /var → 1.4G. du -sh monitor/ → 16M.
infrastructure_findings.root_cause: System image baseline (/usr 6.1G) plus /var growth (logs, package cache). Monitor append-only directories are NOT the primary growth vector (only 16MB). The git gc --auto addition may help if pack files in /var/... are contributing.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: The disk trajectory is concerning but the new git gc --auto should help. If %use crosses 85% tomorrow, escalate to human. Consider /var cleanup (apt cache, logs) as a future PROP if gc doesn't bend the curve.

recommendations.description: Apply PROP-007 urgently. FUSE staleness has expanded from 2 files (yesterday) to 4 files today. build.js itself now has a 3rd different GitHub hash that the workspace has never seen. Every publish cycle runs without git gc --auto (the workspace build.js predates that addition). The staleness class is growing, not shrinking.

generate-html.js and docs/index.html ARE in OWNERSHIP but are still stale. This is a DIFFERENT bug from PROP-007. Either no publish has run since their latest commits, or the publish sync step is silently failing for these files. The next decider publish should verify these files sync. If they remain stale after the next publish, the sync step needs debugging.

PROP-005 (decider pre-flight work check) — 10th consecutive STILL_BROKEN. Either merge the prompt change to save ~700 lines/no-op-run (42% of runs), or close as won't-fix so tinker stops re-escalating.
